title: Haddy the Mouse and the Sparkly Bissolam
summary: Haddy the Mouse embarks on a creative journey to make a magnificent piece of sparkly art, complete with jingle bells and balloons, for her friends Victor, Mr. Slinger, and Lily. As she prepares for the Bissolam—a joyful day of dancing and music—Haddy visits her baby sister, Oscar Mouse, and several other tiny friends at the hospital's baby lounge. When Oscar finally returns home, she finds herself missing the crackers and colors of the lounge. Haddy comforts her with songs and the promise of home-made drawings, eventually singing her baby sister into a peaceful sleep.

highlightQuote.text: No, it's going to be a picture that where you dance. The Bissolam. It's the Bissolam. That's a special day when we dance.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Haddy, Haddy, Haddy, Haddy the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, how does it start?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First he has an idea by going to sleep. It's a sweet story about Haddy the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was her idea?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She wants to work on art.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she wanted to work on art. What kind of art?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The art that I want to make. For first day, for the first day, it's nice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now what kind of art? Is it going to be a sculpture or a drawing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's going to be sparkly art with jingle bells, balloons, and pictures.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And everything else. You know that? And decoration.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That sounds like an amazing piece of art. And so, once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where'd you put Haddy the mouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm holding Haddy while you run around in circles.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's continue the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Once upon a time, Haddy the mouse decided to make an incredible piece of art. It was going to be sparkly, it was going to be big, it was going to be beautiful. But she had to decide who she was going to make her piece of art for. Who did she want to make it for?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor, Mr. Slinger, and Lily.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: For all of those people. Victor, Mr. Slinger, and Lily. And so then she had to decide what was it going to be about? What kind of a picture was it? Was it going to be a picture of a person or a place or a thing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's going to be a picture that where you dance. The Bissolam. It's the Bissolam. That's a special day when we dance.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Bissolam is a special day when we dance. I didn't know that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's the day when we twirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and does it have music too, the Bissolam?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And could you sing some of that music for me please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I don't know how it goes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Bissolam has very nice music. What's the story about Haddy the mouse going to the Bissolam?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: To the Bissolam. Well...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With the music and where you twirl and it has crackers and the lounge where she, the baby lounge where Haddy the mouse sits and around the chairs, the grey chairs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they have colors and we sit and you can color. And Haddy the mouse colors with his friend Lily. That's where he used to color, at the baby lounge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's just like the family lounge at the NICU. I see. Well, as it turned out, Haddy the mouse also had a baby sister who had to stay at the hospital for a while. What was Haddy the mouse's baby sister's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where'd they put Haddy the mouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Behind you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Behind me? Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, over by the dresser there.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bam!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bam.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you're not doing the story. Why are you not doing the story?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm not sure what should happen next. I was thinking about Haddy the mouse's sparkly piece of art and the Bissolam where you twirl and dance to the music and the family lounge that has crackers. There's lots of interesting parts of our story. I'm not sure what should happen next. Maybe Haddy the mouse is going to go visit her baby sister.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: At the baby lounge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where you can do... where Haddy the mouse can do lots of kinds of things. Haddy the mouse, that's a story about me, Squeaker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sweet Squeaker? That's a story about me, said Haddy the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and it's a story about you and the baby lounge and doing lots of things there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did Haddy the mouse really have a baby sister?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was her name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And her name was Baby Oscar Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Baby Oscar Mouse was Haddy's baby sister. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oscar Mouse and a new baby called Alfie and Mickey and Arthur and Minnie and Mitchy and New Oscar too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are those all babies at the hospital?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep. Oscar... no, no, Haddy the mouse can pick which one to bring home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so who did she pick?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oscar. And Haddy the mouse went to her babies and they said hello, we are happy to see you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what else did they say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They said, how are you today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where'd they put Haddy the mouse again?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'll give you Haddy back. And what else happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They went home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so Haddy the mouse brought his baby sister, brought her baby sister Oscar back home.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But then when Oscar was at home, Oscar said, "I want to go back to the hospital and to the lounge and eat crackers."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but Haddy the mouse said, "Well, Oscar, you're home now with us. It's much better than being at the hospital because we have everything you could ever want to play with here, and also plenty of crackers."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We even have crayons so you could draw if you wanted to.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't want to. I want to go back to the hospital.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, we can visit there because the very nice nurses and doctors would love to see us. Should we go visit right now?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or we could take photos of them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we could take photos, yeah, that's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or we should play songs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good idea. Let's play a song.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Baby Oscar Mouse, I love you. I can sing you a song that you can do all by yourself.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and that's the blue timer. So that song's come to the end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oscar, did you like my song? You can do it, Oscar Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Baby Oscar Mouse says, "Wow, I loved that song." And then he went to sleep. Zzzzz. And that's the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now let's turn on our light and we'll turn out the stars.
